note_data.title: Preparation of salts
keyPoints: Filtration separates insoluble solid from liquid - liquid particles small enough to go through filter paper pores, solid particles too large
Crystallization is used for obtaining solid from solid & liquid solution - produces crystals of pure solid on cooling
Distillation obtains pure liquid from solution - pure liquid has low BP and changes to gas easily, solid has high BP and remains in flask
Fractional distillation separates 2 liquids which are mixed (miscible) - liquid with lowest BP comes out first at top of fractionating column
Chromatography separates mixture of organic compounds like colour dyes
Solid obtained in filtration is called 'Residue', liquid obtained is called 'Filtrate'
In crystallization process: dissolve solid in solvent, heat solution to evaporate solvent, produces hot saturated solution, crystals form on cooling
In distillation, liquid obtained is called 'Distillate' and is collected in conical flask
Pure substances have fixed composition, fixed Mp/Bp, produce only 1 spot on chromatogram
Mixtures have variable composition, variable Bp/Mp, produce 2 or more spots on chromatogram

definitions.definition: no more solid can dissolve in it
Formation of crystals from a cooling liquid/ Saturated solution

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give a step-by-step method with apparatus and observations
Give a suitable method based on whether salt is soluble or insoluble

modelAnswer.question: Describe how to prepare pure, dry crystals of copper sulfate from copper oxide and sulfuric acid.
modelAnswer.answer: Add excess copper oxide to dilute sulfuric acid and stir until no more dissolves. Filter to remove unreacted copper oxide. Heat the filtrate to concentrate the solution. Allow to cool and copper sulfate crystals will form. Filter and wash with distilled water, then dry between filter papers.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Add excess copper oxide to acid
Mark 2: Filter to remove unreacted solid
Mark 4: Cool to form crystals and dry

commonMistakes: Confusing when to use crystallization vs distillation - crystallization obtains solid, distillation obtains liquid
Not understanding that filtration only works because solid particles are too large for filter paper pores
Mixing up the products - residue is solid from filtration, filtrate is liquid, distillate is from distillation
examinerTips: Any question that wants the method to obtain (make) crystals, the process will be 'Crystallization'
The constant temperature of distillation process equals the Boiling Point of pure liquid
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Crystallisation setup showing evaporating dish on tripod and gauze over Bunsen burner, with watch glass covering dish and glass rod for stirring
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to describe the preparation of copper sulfate crystals from copper carbonate, including purification steps
O/N 2022 P4 Q2 (8 marks) examined the preparation of zinc chloride from zinc oxide and hydrochloric acid, requiring students to explain filtration and crystallization
M/J 2024 P2 Q3 (5 marks) tested preparation of lead chloride by precipitation, asking for the complete method including washing and drying
O/N 2023 P4 Q1 (7 marks) focused on preparing ammonium sulfate from ammonia and sulfuric acid, emphasizing neutralization indicators and crystal formation

workedExample.question: Describe how to prepare pure, dry crystals of copper sulfate from copper carbonate and sulfuric acid. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Add copper carbonate powder to dilute sulfuric acid in a beaker, stirring continuously
Step 2: Continue adding copper carbonate until no more dissolves (excess remains) - effervescence stops
Step 3: Filter the mixture to remove excess copper carbonate using filter paper and funnel
Step 4: Heat the filtrate gently to evaporate water until crystals start to form on a glass rod
Step 5: Allow the solution to cool slowly for large crystals to form
Step 6: Filter to collect crystals and wash with distilled water, then dry between filter papers
workedExample.answer: Add excess copper carbonate to dilute sulfuric acid until reaction stops. Filter to remove unreacted solid. Gently heat filtrate until saturated, then cool to crystallize. Filter, wash and dry crystals.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark each for: excess reactant, filtration, gentle heating/evaporation, cooling for crystallization, washing crystals, drying method

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates wrote 'boil to dryness' instead of gentle heating' — always use gentle heating/evaporation to avoid decomposing the salt when preparing crystals
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often forgot to mention washing crystals' — always state that crystals must be washed with distilled water to remove impurities before drying
Examiner Report 2024: 'Candidates confused precipitation with crystallization methods' — use precipitation for insoluble salts, crystallization for soluble salts from solution
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must draw the crystallization setup showing: beaker containing salt solution on tripod with gauze, Bunsen burner underneath for gentle heating, glass rod for testing saturation point, and evaporation dish as alternative. Include labels for 'gentle heating', 'saturated solution', and 'point where crystals form on glass rod'. For filtration, show funnel with filter paper, residue on paper, and filtrate collected below.
note_data.quickRecap: Separation methods depend on physical properties: filtration for insoluble solids uses particle size differences, crystallization obtains pure solid crystals from solutions, distillation obtains pure liquids using boiling point differences, and fractional distillation separates miscible liquids. Products have specific names: residue (solid from filtration), filtrate (liquid from filtration), and distillate (liquid from distillation).
